summary: Deep research + adversarial verification for P2 gap partition 8 (21 models)
logs: research: 21 models researched, 0 rejected as unconfirmable
verify: 21 models confirmed, 0 rejected by verification

models.name: GPT-Neo 2.7B
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer, alternating local (256-token window) and global sparse attention (GPT-3 replication)
models.note: First open GPT-3-style replication trained on The Pile; largest freely available autoregressive LM at its March 2021 launch
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: fetched huggingface.co/EleutherAI/gpt-neo-2.7B model card table verbatim-matches all 4 scores (LAMBADA 62.22, Winogrande 56.50, HellaSwag 42.73, PIQA 72.14). MIT license and 2021-03-21 release date (eleuther.ai/artifacts/gpt-neo shows '21 Mar') confirmed. No changes needed.

models.name: GPT-J-6B
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer with Rotary Position Embeddings and parallel attention+FF blocks (mesh-transformer-jax)
models.note: 6B Pile-trained model built with mesh-transformer-jax, Apache 2.0; the standard open GPT-3 alternative for roughly two years
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: fetched huggingface.co/EleutherAI/gpt-j-6b model card, exact param count 6,053,381,344 = 6.05B matches record; Apache-2.0 license and 2048 context confirmed; evaluation table verbatim-matches all 4 scores. Wikipedia confirms release date June 9, 2021. No changes needed.

models.name: GPT-NeoX-20B
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer with RoPE and parallel attention+FF blocks (GPT-NeoX library, 44 layers)
models.note: Largest publicly available dense LM at its Feb 2022 release; trained on The Pile with the GPT-NeoX framework, Apache 2.0

results.notes: average across Humanities/Social Science/STEM subject clusters; one of the earliest models eval'd on then-new MMLU

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: fetched blog.eleuther.ai/announcing-20b/ - all 4 scores (LAMBADA 72.00, Winogrande 66.10, HellaSwag 53.50, MMLU avg 28.98) verbatim-match. HF model card gives exact param count 20,554,567,680 which rounds to 20.6B, matching record. Apache-2.0 and 2022-02-09 release date confirmed. No changes needed.

models.name: Pythia-1.4B
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer (GPT-NeoX architecture, RoPE, parallel attention+FF, 24 layers)
models.note: Smaller checkpoint in the 8-model Pythia interpretability suite, identical data/order across all scales
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: fetched huggingface.co/EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b - exact param count 1,414,647,808 (=1.41B, rounds to 1.4 matching record), 24 layers, Apache-2.0 license confirmed. Training used 2048 sequence length (ctxK=2 confirmed via arxiv paper details). Paper submission date 2023-04-03 confirmed on arxiv. No results rows to verify (none were provided).

models.name: Pythia-6.9B
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer (GPT-NeoX architecture, RoPE, parallel attention+FF, 32 layers)
models.note: Mid-large Pythia size widely used as a reproducible base for training-dynamics and scaling-law studies
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: fetched huggingface.co/EleutherAI/pythia-6.9b - exact param count 6,857,302,016 (=6.857B, rounds to 6.9 matching record), 32 layers, Apache-2.0 license confirmed. 2048 sequence length confirmed. Paper date 2023-04-03 confirmed on arxiv. No results rows to verify (none were provided).

models.name: Pythia-12B
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer (GPT-NeoX architecture, RoPE, parallel attention+FF, 36 layers)
models.note: Flagship of the 8-model Pythia suite; 154 released checkpoints per model for interpretability/training-dynamics research

results.notes: zero-shot accuracy, LM Eval Harness, lambada_openai task, final checkpoint (step143000) — raw eval JSON acc=0.7046
zero-shot, LM Eval Harness, final checkpoint (step143000) — raw eval JSON acc=0.7601
zero-shot, LM Eval Harness, final checkpoint (step143000) — raw eval JSON acc=0.7024

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: exact param count 11,846,072,320 (=11.846B, rounds to 11.8 matching record), 36 layers, Apache-2.0. Original sourceUrl (arxiv abstract page) does not literally display these numbers, so I re-sourced them: fetched the official raw eval JSON from github.com/EleutherAI/pythia (evals/pythia-v1/pythia-12b/zero-shot/12b_step143000.json, the final-checkpoint zero-shot run) and confirmed lambada_openai acc=0.7046(→70.5), piqa acc=0.7601(→76.0), arc_easy acc=0.7024(→70.2) — all match the claimed scores essentially exactly. Updated each row's sourceUrl to this verifiable GitHub JSON.

models.name: MiniCPM-2B
models.license: Custom (OpenBMB General Model License; free for research, free commercial use after registration)
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer, deep-and-thin design (40 layers, 2304 hidden dim, Mistral-derived tokenizer)
models.note: Pioneering ~2.4B-non-embedding edge LLM matching Mistral-7B on many benchmarks; runs on-device including smartphones

results.notes: MiniCPM GitHub README benchmark table; Chinese exam benchmark
MiniCPM GitHub README benchmark table; Chinese MMLU analogue

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model via HF model card (huggingface.co/openbmb/MiniCPM-2B-sft-bf16, 2.4B non-embedding params, Apache-2.0 code / custom GML weights license, Feb 1 2024 release corroborated by multiple independent sources). All 11 benchmark scores independently re-fetched from the OpenBMB/MiniCPM GitHub README table and match the input exactly (MMLU 53.46, BBH 36.87, GSM8K 53.83, MATH 10.24, HumanEval 50.00, MBPP 47.31, HellaSwag 68.25, ARC-C 68.00, ARC-E 85.44, C-Eval 51.13, CMMLU 51.07); swapped sourceUrl from the arxiv abstract page (which does not display the table) to the GitHub README that actually contains it, since arxiv abstract pages don't literally show these numbers.

models.name: MiniCPM-Llama3-V 2.5
models.license: MiniCPM Model License (custom; free for research and, after registration, commercial use) + Apache-2.0 code
models.archDisplay: SigLip-400M vision encoder + Llama3-8B-Instruct LLM
models.note: First end-side 8B MLLM to reach GPT-4V level, surpassing Gemini Pro and Claude 3 on OpenCompass (65.1 avg).

results.notes: OpenCompass average over 11 benchmarks; surpasses GPT-4V-1106, Gemini Pro, Claude 3

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real, independently re-fetched HF model card (openbmb/MiniCPM-Llama3-V-2_5): 8B params (SigLip-400M + Llama3-8B-Instruct), MiniCPM Model License weights / Apache-2.0 code, released 2024-05-20 as stated on the model card. Context length confirmed as 8192 (8k) directly from config.json max_position_embeddings, correcting an unverified assumption to a directly-confirmed primary-source value. OpenCompass 65.1 average score confirmed present verbatim on the HF model card.

models.name: MiniCPM-V 2.6
models.license: MiniCPM Model License (custom; free for research and, after registration, commercial use) + Apache-2.0 code
models.archDisplay: SigLip-400M vision encoder + Qwen2-7B LLM
models.note: 8B MLLM adding multi-image and video understanding on-device, surpassing GPT-4V/Gemini 1.5 Pro on OpenCompass (65.2 avg).

results.notes: OpenCompass average over 8 benchmarks; surpasses GPT-4o mini, GPT-4V, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Claude 3.5 Sonnet on single-image understanding

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real, independently re-fetched HF model card (openbmb/MiniCPM-V-2_6): 8B params (SigLip-400M + Qwen2-7B), MiniCPM Model License weights / Apache-2.0 code. 32k context window independently confirmed via multiple sources (HF card, MarkTechPost coverage) as a first-class feature (multi-image + video understanding), not an unverified guess. Paper/GitHub dated August 2024 (arxiv paper timestamp Aug 3 2024; release announcements early August), consistent with the 2024-08-06 release date. OpenCompass 65.2 average confirmed present verbatim on the HF model card.

models.name: MiniCPM3-4B
models.license: MiniCPM Model License (custom; free for research and, after registration, commercial use) + Apache-2.0 code
models.archDisplay: Dense causal transformer (third-gen MiniCPM)
models.note: Third-gen 4B on-device LLM with function calling and code interpreter, outperforming GPT-3.5-Turbo-0125 and Phi-3.5-mini.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real, independently re-fetched HF model card: 4B params, 32k native context, MiniCPM Model License (weights) + Apache-2.0 (code), function calling + code interpreter support. Release date 2024-09-05 corroborated by MarkTechPost coverage (2024-09-11) and OpenBMB's own announcement tweet; all five benchmark numbers (MMLU 67.2, GSM8K 81.1, HumanEval+ 68.3, BFCL v2 76.0, MT-Bench 8.41) confirmed present on the HF model card.

models.name: MiniCPM-o 2.6
models.license: MiniCPM Model License (custom; free for research and, after registration, commercial use) + Apache-2.0 code
models.archDisplay: SigLip-400M + Whisper-medium-300M + ChatTTS-200M + Qwen2.5-7B, end-to-end omni model
models.note: 8B any-to-any omni model (vision+speech+language) with real-time streaming, matching GPT-4o-202405 on OpenCompass (70.2 avg).

results.notes: OpenCompass average over 8 benchmarks; surpasses GPT-4o-202405, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Claude 3.5 Sonnet on single-image understanding
Outperforms GPT-4o-202408 (64.1) on multimodal live-streaming benchmark

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model via HF model card (8B total, SigLip-400M+Whisper-medium-300M+ChatTTS-200M+Qwen2.5-7B, MiniCPM Model License weights / Apache-2.0 code, max_position_embeddings=32768 -> 32k ctx, independently confirmed). Release corroborated: OpenBMB announced via X on Jan 13, 2025 with MarkTechPost/press covering it Jan 14, 2025 (1-day spread reflects announcement-vs-press-coverage timing, not a factual error) -- kept 2025-01-13 as the announcement date. OpenCompass 70.2, OCRBench 897, and StreamingBench 66.0 all confirmed present on the HF model card.

models.name: iFlytek Spark V1.0 (Xinghuo)
models.archDisplay: Proprietary transformer (undisclosed)
models.note: iFlytek's first Spark cognitive model, one of China's earliest ChatGPT challengers; launched with speech I/O, image understanding added later in 2023.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real launch event: multiple independent sources (Baidu Baike, chinaz.com announcement of the May 6 2023 release conference, and general web search corroboration) confirm iFlytek held its 'Xinghuo (Spark) Cognitive Large Model' achievement release conference on 2023-05-06, matching the record. The originally-cited news.cri.cn source could not be re-confirmed (it discusses a later SuperCLUE ranking, not the initial release), so it was replaced with a source that directly corroborates the release date; params/architecture remain correctly undisclosed (closed proprietary model), and no benchmark result rows were included, consistent with none being verifiable for this early proprietary release.

models.name: iFlytek Spark V3.0 (Xinghuo)
models.archDisplay: Proprietary transformer (undisclosed)
models.note: Third-gen Spark benchmarked against ChatGPT-3.5, unveiled at the 2023 iFlytek 1024 Developer Festival in Hefei; later rebranded as the API's 'Spark Pro' tier.

results.notes: SuperCLUE overall score for 讯飞星火V3.0 in the Dec 2023 leaderboard (also 51.74 on OPEN sub-task, 77.03 on OPT sub-task)

models.verificationNotes: Existence and Oct 24, 2023 Hefei release confirmed via chinadaily.com.cn, iflytek.com, and multiple press (PRNewswire, Pandaily). SuperCLUE score of 59.33 (plus 51.74 OPEN / 77.03 OPT sub-scores) for Dec 2023 confirmed by fetching github.com/CLUEbenchmark/SuperCLUE directly - numbers match exactly. 'Rebranded as Spark Pro' claim is plausible per Chinese AI ecosystem reporting but not independently pinned to a single primary source; left in note field (non-critical, unverified nuance) rather than a hard spec.

models.name: iFlytek Spark V4.0 (Xinghuo)
models.archDisplay: Proprietary transformer, trained fully on domestic (Huawei Ascend) compute
models.note: GPT-4-Turbo-class flagship trained on fully domestic compute; later refreshed as Spark 4.0 Turbo in October 2024.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via TechNode, Caixin Global, ChinaDaily, and IoT Now: launched June 27, 2024 in Beijing, positioned as rivaling/surpassing GPT-4 Turbo on several benchmarks, trained exclusively on the Huawei-cobuilt domestic compute platform (no Nvidia dependency) due to US chip export restrictions. Spark 4.0 Turbo follow-up in Oct 2024 also confirmed. No results rows were present to verify.

models.name: iFlytek Spark X1 (Xinghuo X1)
models.license: Proprietary (closed API access, no weight release)
models.archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts transformer (exact total/active params undisclosed)
models.note: iFlytek's first deep-reasoning model, co-developed with Huawei and trained entirely on domestic Ascend 910B compute with no Nvidia dependency.
models.verificationNotes: Existence, Jan 15, 2025 release, and Huawei Ascend 910B domestic-compute training confirmed independently via CGTN, SCMP, and Huawei Central (910B efficiency raised from ~20% to ~80% of Nvidia). 128K context confirmed via a separate aibase.com report on the Spark Deep Reasoning X1. However, neither CGTN, SCMP, nor Huawei Central discloses architecture type or parameter count; one secondary source (TMTPost) claims '70 billion parameters' but this could not be corroborated by any primary source and conflicts with other undisclosed-params reporting, so paramsB is kept null and the MoE archClass claim should be treated as lower-confidence/unconfirmed rather than a hard fact.

models.name: Luminous Supreme (70B)
models.license: Proprietary (Aleph Alpha API Terms & Conditions, SaaS-only)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only autoregressive transformer with rotary position embeddings
models.note: Germany's first large-scale sovereign LLM; API-only 70B flagship of the original Luminous family, now a legacy offering behind PhariaAI.
models.verificationNotes: Release date (Aleph Alpha press release, April 14, 2022, Heidelberg) and 70B parameter count for the Supreme tier of the 13B/30B/70B Luminous family confirmed via multiple independent sources (innFactory, CRFM Stanford FMTI report). Dense decoder-only transformer with rotary position embeddings architecture confirmed. 2048-token context length confirmed as the legacy Luminous spec. Pricing of $175 input / $192.5 output per 1M tokens confirmed by fetching cloudprice.net directly - matches exactly. Deprecated/legacy status is consistent with Aleph Alpha's late-2024 pivot away from marketing Luminous to new customers in favor of PhariaAI/Pharia-1.

models.name: Pharia-1-LLM-7B (control)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer, 27 layers, 4-head grouped-query attention
models.note: Open, EU-compliance-focused 7B trained on lawfully sourced data; Aleph Alpha's strategic successor to Luminous.
models.verificationNotes: Existence and Aug 26, 2024 release confirmed via aleph-alpha.com blog post and corroborating coverage (VentureBeat, Maginative, dataphoenix, and Aleph Alpha's own X/Twitter announcement). 7.04B params (7,041,544,704 exact) and Open Aleph License (non-commercial research/educational use) confirmed by fetching the HF model card directly. All three benchmark scores (MMLU 48.4, HellaSwag 64.6, GSM8K 1.4) confirmed to literally appear on huggingface.co/Aleph-Alpha/Pharia-1-LLM-7B-control - exact match, no changes needed.

models.name: H2O-Danube 1.8B
models.archDisplay: Dense Llama-2/Mistral-style transformer with sliding-window attention + GQA
models.note: H2O.ai's debut small LLM, Llama-style architecture, tuned for efficient on-device/mobile inference.
models.verificationNotes: Existence and Jan 30, 2024 release confirmed via arxiv.org/abs/2401.16818 and VentureBeat coverage. 1.8B params, 16,384 context length, and Mistral-style sliding-window attention (4096) confirmed by fetching the HF model card directly. Apache 2.0 license confirmed. All four benchmark scores (ARC-Challenge 39.25, HellaSwag 67.6, Winogrande 65.35, TriviaQA 36.29) confirmed to literally appear on huggingface.co/h2oai/h2o-danube-1.8b-chat - exact match, no changes needed.

models.name: H2O-Danube3 4B
models.archDisplay: Dense Llama-2-style transformer, wide architecture, GQA (24 layers, 3840 hidden)
models.note: Apache-2.0 small model trained on 6T tokens, compact enough to run locally on a modern smartphone.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via HF model card fetch (h2oai/h2o-danube3-4b-chat): 4B params (modified Llama-2 arch, 24 layers, 3840 hidden, GQA), Apache 2.0, 8192-token context (Mistral tokenizer, 32K vocab), release date matches arXiv 2407.09276 (submitted 2024-07-12). All 5 Open LLM Leaderboard v1 scores (MMLU 54.74, HellaSwag 80.36, ARC-C 58.96, GSM8K 50.18, Winogrande 76.48) literally verified present on the HF page. No changes needed.

models.name: WizardLM 7B
models.archDisplay: Dense LLaMA-based decoder-only transformer
models.note: Original WizardLM: LLaMA fine-tuned on Evol-Instruct data, a method that seeded WizardCoder/WizardMath and much of the field.

results.notes: pass@1, per original WizardLM-7B-V1.0 model card
AlpacaEval win rate (%) vs text-davinci-003 baseline, per original model card table

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: arXiv 2304.12244 (submitted 2023-04-24) lists authors Can Xu, Qingfeng Sun, Kai Zheng, Xiubo Geng, Pu Zhao, Jiazhan Feng, Chongyang Tao, Qingwei Lin, Daxin Jiang, affiliated 1=Microsoft, 2=Peking University — org confirmed. Corrected: the official HF repo is WizardLM/WizardLM-7B-V1.0 (input had non-existent 'WizardLMTeam/WizardLM-7B-V1.0'; that org handle exists for the 13B/Math models but not this 7B one, and it 401'd/does not resolve). Corrected: the '78' score is NOT a 'vicuna-eval win rate vs Vicuna-13B' as the input claimed — the TheBloke-mirrored model card literally labels it 'AlpacaEval' win rate against the text-davinci-003 baseline, so I relabeled benchmarkSlug to alpacaeval and fixed the notes; HumanEval 19.1 pass@1 confirmed verbatim on the same page. Context length 2K is consistent with the LLaMA-1 base (2048 tokens) used in April 2023. License is not machine-readable on HF (tagged 'other') but is widely documented as non-commercial/research-only, inherited from Meta's original LLaMA-1 research license — kept with lower confidence.

models.name: Krutrim-2 (12B)
models.archDisplay: Dense Mistral-NeMo-based transformer, 40 layers, GQA (32 heads / 8 KV heads)
models.note: 12B Indic LLM built on Mistral-NeMo; 128K context spanning English plus 22 Indian languages.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via HF model card fetch (krutrim-ai-labs/Krutrim-2-instruct): 12B dense Mistral-NeMo-based model, 40 layers, 5120 hidden dim, GQA 32 heads/8 KV heads, 128K context, 131,072 vocab, released 2025-01-31, license 'Krutrim Community License'. Natively supports English + 22 Indian languages (23 total), matching langCount. All 5 benchmark rows (MMLU 63, HumanEval Pass@10 80, GSM8K 71, HellaSwag 83, Winogrande 77) literally verified present on the HF page. apiAvailable=true confirmed via Krutrim Cloud platform (HF page itself notes no HF Inference Provider deployment, but model is served on Krutrim's own cloud/API). No changes needed.
